message:
perf(core): gate StatementLookasideGrowthGuard::new capture on profile flag (OPT-B1)

The growth guard is constructed on every prepared-DML call (three call
sites in execute_prepared_direct_simple_insert and its UPDATE/DELETE
siblings). Its `before_bytes` is read via five chained RefCell::borrow()s
across scratch buffers, but the drop-side `note_statement_lookaside_alloc_
growth` already early-exits when `hot_path_profile_enabled()` returns
false — so in release builds that value is computed and then discarded.

Gate the capture behind the same runtime flag. When profiling is off we
skip the five borrows outright; when profiling is on we still capture a
snapshot. A sample that straddles a mid-call profile toggle will read
growth=0 once, which is acceptable for a diagnostic counter.

Measurable impact is inside run-to-run noise on perf_update_delete
(per-row-update 7507-7625 ns vs 7550-7625 ns pre-change), which is expected
given how cheap `RefCell::borrow()` already is on a single-owner scratch;
the change is still a strict cleanup since the captured value was unused.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
